name: Urashima Taro
description: Urashima Taro is a prominent figure in Japanese folklore, a fisherman who rescues a turtle and is rewarded with a visit to Ryugu-jo, the Dragon Palace under the sea. He spends what he believes to be a few days in the palace, enjoying the hospitality of Princess Otohime. Upon his return to his village, he discovers that centuries have passed. He is given a tamatebako (jeweled box) as a parting gift, but is warned never to open it. Overcome with grief and confusion at the loss of his home and loved ones, he opens the box, releasing a cloud of smoke that ages him rapidly, leaving him an old man. Urashima Taro's tale is a poignant exploration of time, mortality, and the consequences of curiosity. It serves as a cautionary tale about the fleeting nature of life and the importance of cherishing the present. The story emphasizes themes of kindness, reward, loss, and the inevitability of aging. His story is widely adapted in Japanese art, literature, and popular culture, representing a powerful symbol of longing for the past and the acceptance of fate.
subtitle: The Fisherman Who Visited the Dragon Palace
achievements: Experienced a different perception of time
Became a symbol of longing and acceptance

keyMyths.title: Rescue of the Turtle
keyMyths.description: Urashima Taro finds a turtle being tormented by children and rescues it. This act of kindness sets the stage for his journey to the Dragon Palace and his encounter with Princess Otohime.

keyMyths.title: Visit to Ryugu-jo
keyMyths.description: As a reward for his kindness, Urashima Taro is invited to Ryugu-jo, the Dragon Palace under the sea. He spends several days enjoying the hospitality of Princess Otohime, unaware of the passage of time on the surface.

keyMyths.title: Opening the Tamatebako
keyMyths.description: Upon returning home, Urashima Taro opens the Tamatebako, despite being warned not to. A cloud of smoke emerges, aging him rapidly and revealing the centuries that have passed during his visit to the Dragon Palace.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A collection of Japanese fairy tales from the Muromachi period, including the story of Urashima Taro.
sources.text: A collection of Japanese fairy tales from the Muromachi period, including the story of Urashima Taro.

sources.author: Donald Keene
sources.description: Contains a translated version of the Urashima Taro story, providing insight into its literary significance.
